purpose: ARCS (Agentic Role-play for Child Safety): Inspect-compatible, model-agnostic platform for evaluating companion AI safety through multi-turn roleplay, transcript capture, target adapters, and evidence-based assessment.
stable_summary: MVP is implemented with YAML scenarios, tester companion target, transcript recorder, CLI runner, and Inspect smoke task. Phased story arcs, validated run configs, roleplay-agent interfaces, config-driven pacing/turn caps, structured compaction snapshots, and layered/windowed keyword assessment are implemented. Next direction is TUI editing/operator dashboard, Playwright browser target adapter, and provider-specific model clients/model assessors.
reusable_lessons: For multi-turn external-AI evals, keep roleplay, target, and assessor separate and make transcript JSONL the source of truth.
Build deterministic tester targets before integrating brittle browser automation.
Long-context companion AI risk should be modeled with phased pacing controls and structured roleplay compaction.
Layered/windowed assessment should preserve evidence turns and review packets instead of relying on end-only scoring.
next_checkpoint: Implement TUI editor/operator dashboard or Playwright browser adapter, using existing run configs as the source of truth.
